hero.title: Is Türkiye the Right Move for You?
hero.body: Dynamic Residency is an Istanbul-based cross-border residency planning service. We help entrepreneurs, investors and internationally connected families understand how Turkish residence may affect their income, companies, investments and family plans — before irreversible decisions are made.
hero.note: A project of Dynamic Works Türkiye

about.title: Why Dynamic Residency Exists
about.lead: Dynamic Residency helps answer one practical question: does Türkiye work for your individual situation?
about.body1: Most information focuses on tax advantages, immigration or residence permits. A sound decision also depends on your current tax residence, companies, investments, income sources, family circumstances and long-term objectives.
about.body2: We coordinate those connected questions to create a clear decision path.
about.statement1: Not to persuade you to relocate.
about.statement2: To help you decide whether relocating is right for you.

opportunity.title: Türkiye's 20-Year Foreign Income Tax Exemption
opportunity.body1: Eligible individuals who become Turkish tax residents from 2026 may qualify for an exemption on certain foreign-source income for up to 20 years.
opportunity.body2: The exemption may be relevant to individuals receiving:
opportunity.noticeBody: Income paid from abroad is not automatically foreign-source income. Eligibility depends on your residence history, the actual source of the income and your wider cross-border structure.

founder.title: Meet the Founder
founder.name: Stephan Westermayer
roleLines: Founder & CEO of Dynamic Works and Blue Palm Group
founder.bio: Stephan is personally involved in the strategic direction of Dynamic Residency and selected complex client cases. Standard consultations are handled by the appropriate members of the Dynamic Residency professional network.
origin: I could have built my companies in many places. I chose Istanbul deliberately — not to leave the world behind, but to remain connected to it.

I continue to spend time in the United States and travel internationally. Türkiye became my operational base because it combines connectivity, entrepreneurial opportunity, talent and quality of life in a way I have not experienced elsewhere.
Dynamic Residency grew out of that experience — not from studying Türkiye at a distance, but from living and building here.
founder.imageAlt: Stephan Westermayer, Founder of Dynamic Residency

hero.title: Cross-Border Residency Planning for Türkiye, Built Around the Whole Structure
hero.lead: Dynamic Residency is an Istanbul-based cross-border residency planning and implementation-coordination platform for entrepreneurs, investors, internationally active professionals, returning Turkish citizens and globally mobile families considering Türkiye.
hero.body1: We help clients understand how Turkish residence may interact with foreign income, company ownership, investments, property, family circumstances and long-term objectives — before decisions become difficult or expensive to reverse.
hero.body2: Our role is to establish the relevant facts, connect the questions that belong together, coordinate qualified professional input where regulated advice is required and turn the results into a clear decision and implementation path.
hero.small: Based in Istanbul | English and German service | Confidential cross-border process

whatIs.title: Residence Is Never an Isolated Decision
whatIs.body1: Dynamic Residency is a specialised service platform focused on the economic and structural consequences of establishing residence in Türkiye. We do not treat residence as an isolated permit, a single tax rule or a destination choice. We examine the complete international situation in which the residence decision must operate.
whatIs.body2: That situation may include the country being left, current and future tax residence, foreign-source and Turkish-source income, companies and management activity, investments, property, family links, succession considerations and the practical steps required to implement a viable structure.
whatIs.body3: The objective is not to persuade every client to move to Türkiye. The objective is to determine whether Türkiye is suitable for the client's actual circumstances and, where it is suitable, to coordinate the route from analysis to implementation.

whyExists.title: Close the Gap Between Information and a Decision That Works
whyExists.body1: International relocation is often explained through separate topics: residence permits, tax rates, property, company formation or lifestyle. In practice, the outcome depends on how those topics interact across more than one jurisdiction.
whyExists.body2: A decision that appears attractive in Türkiye may create an unintended result in the country being left. A foreign company may be affected by where it is managed. Income paid from abroad may not be foreign-source. Family ties, travel patterns or business activity may change the residence analysis. Timing may determine whether an opportunity remains available.
whyExists.body3: Dynamic Residency was created to close the gap between general information and a decision that can operate in real life. We begin while the important variables can still be managed — before residence changes, company management moves, assets are sold, ownership is restructured or family arrangements become difficult to reverse.

questions.title: One Connected Structure
items: Current and future tax residence, domicile and physical-presence patterns
The legal and economic source of dividends, interest, rent, pensions, capital gains and professional income
Foreign companies, shareholdings, management activity and permanent-establishment exposure
Property, investment portfolios, banking relationships and cross-border cash flows
Family residence, education, healthcare, succession and long-term mobility
Departure-country obligations, double-tax treaties and implementation timing
The evidence, professional opinions and practical steps required before a decision is executed
questions.outro: These questions are considered as one connected structure. The purpose is to prevent a technically correct answer in one area from creating a practical problem somewhere else.

exemptionTeaser.title: Türkiye's 20-Year Foreign Income Tax Exemption
exemptionTeaser.body: Türkiye has introduced a potentially significant exemption for eligible individuals who become Turkish tax residents from 2026. The rule may be relevant to certain foreign-source income, but qualification depends on residence history, the true source of the income, the application process and the wider cross-border structure. It should therefore be reviewed as part of the complete residence decision, not as a stand-alone promise.
exemptionTeaser.cta: Understand Türkiye's 20-Year Foreign Income Tax Exemption

howItWorks.title: From Starting Position to Implementation

steps.title: Establish the Starting Position
steps.desc: We organise the available facts about residence history, income, companies, assets, family circumstances, travel patterns and objectives.

steps.title: Build the Question Map
steps.desc: We identify the connected commercial, operational, tax, legal, immigration and corporate questions that must be resolved before a decision can be made.

steps.title: Coordinate Qualified Input
steps.desc: Where a formal opinion is required, appropriately qualified professionals address the defined questions within their jurisdiction and field of expertise.

steps.title: Create the Decision Path
steps.desc: Dynamic Residency brings the findings together, identifies dependencies and presents the viable route, material risks and required sequence of actions.

steps.title: Coordinate Implementation
steps.desc: Where the client proceeds, we coordinate the agreed workstreams so that documentation, timing and practical execution remain aligned with the approved structure.

howItWorks.note: A decision gate applies throughout the process. If the structure is not viable, the work stops with a clear explanation. If it is viable, implementation begins only after the route, responsibilities and limitations are understood.

cards.title: The Complete Structure Comes First
cards.desc: We do not begin with a product to sell. We begin with the client's position and the decision that must be made. Residence, income, companies, assets and family circumstances are reviewed together because they can change one another's outcome.

cards.title: Implementation Is Part of the Analysis
cards.desc: A structure is useful only when it can be documented, administered and maintained. Banking, payroll, company management, residence procedures, local operations and professional coordination must function outside a presentation or memorandum.

cards.title: Professional Roles Remain Clear
cards.desc: Dynamic Residency leads the commercial analysis, cross-border coordination and implementation roadmap. Formal tax, legal, immigration and corporate opinions are provided or reviewed by appropriately qualified professionals. Clients may also retain their existing advisers, while we define the Türkiye-side questions and coordinate the combined work.

cards.title: The Decision Is Independent
cards.desc: Türkiye is not recommended by default. A negative conclusion can be the correct result. Our purpose is to create clarity before the client commits capital, changes residence or restructures an existing international position.

realOperations.eyebrow: Built on Real Operations in Türkiye
realOperations.title: Practical Experience, Not Distance
realOperations.body1: Dynamic Residency is a specialised project developed within Dynamic Works Türkiye. Its operating perspective comes from building and managing companies, employing local teams, supporting international entrepreneurs and developing practical business infrastructure in Türkiye.
realOperations.body2: This operating background matters because internationally connected clients do not only need information about Türkiye. They need to understand how a proposed structure will work with people, companies, documentation, banks, advisers, authorities and day-to-day management.
realOperations.body3: Dynamic Residency combines local operating experience with a cross-border professional network. Specialist input is introduced where the question requires regulated expertise, while the overall decision remains coordinated around the client's complete structure.

founderSection.title: Developed by Stephan Westermayer
founderSection.body1: Dynamic Residency was developed by Stephan Westermayer, Founder of Dynamic Residency and Founder and Chief Executive Officer of Dynamic Works and Blue Palm Group. The platform grew from his direct experience of living and operating in Türkiye, building companies, working with local teams and coordinating international structures.
founderSection.body2: Stephan leads the strategic direction of Dynamic Residency and is personally involved in selected complex cases involving business ownership, international assets or several connected jurisdictions. Standard consultations and specialist work are handled by the appropriate members of the professional network.
founderSection.body3: The platform is therefore built from practical experience in Türkiye, but it is designed for decisions that extend beyond Türkiye. The relevant question is not only what Türkiye allows. It is whether the complete international structure works before, during and after the move.

cards.title: Company Owners and Founders
cards.desc: Entrepreneurs who want to establish residence in Türkiye without creating avoidable problems for foreign companies, management activity, payroll, dividends, ownership or operations.

cards.title: International Investors
cards.desc: Individuals receiving dividends, interest, rental income, pensions or capital gains from assets and institutions located in more than one country.

cards.title: Internationally Mobile Families
cards.desc: Families whose residence decision must work with education, healthcare, property, succession, travel and long-term financial planning.

cards.title: Returning Turkish Citizens
cards.desc: Turkish citizens returning after a period abroad who need a structured review of residence history, income, assets and possible eligibility for current Turkish tax provisions.

cards.title: International Professionals and Retirees
cards.desc: Individuals whose work, pension, investments or travel patterns remain international and cannot be understood through a standard domestic relocation process.

cards.title: Professional Advisers
cards.desc: Tax advisers, lawyers, fiduciaries and family-office professionals who require a coordinated Türkiye-side workstream for a client with an existing international structure.

whatNot.title: Clear Professional Boundaries
items: Not a mass-market immigration or residence-permit agency
Not a law firm, tax advisory firm or substitute for regulated professional advice
Not a real estate marketplace, citizenship broker or generic relocation concierge
Not a promise that every client qualifies for a tax exemption
Not a platform that recommends Türkiye regardless of suitability
whatNot.outro: Clear professional boundaries are part of the service. Dynamic Residency coordinates the decision and implementation process while ensuring that formal opinions are obtained from the appropriate qualified professionals.

items.title: Built on Experience
items.desc: Recommendations should be grounded in practical operating reality, not only theory or promotional claims.

items.title: Implementation First
items.desc: A proposed structure must be capable of being documented, administered and maintained after the decision is made.

items.title: Quality Before Growth
items.desc: Complex decisions require disciplined scope, appropriate expertise and sufficient time. Volume is not the objective.

items.title: Transparency
items.desc: Roles, limitations, fees, dependencies and unresolved questions should be clear before work proceeds.

items.title: International by Design
items.desc: Türkiye is considered within the complete cross-border context, including the jurisdictions, companies, assets and family links that remain relevant.

items.a: Dynamic Residency provides cross-border residency planning, structural analysis and implementation coordination for internationally connected people considering Türkiye. It brings together residence, income, company, investment, family and practical implementation questions into one decision process.

items.q: Is Dynamic Residency a law firm or tax advisory firm?
items.a: No. Dynamic Residency leads commercial analysis, question definition, coordination and implementation planning. Formal tax, legal, immigration or corporate opinions are provided or reviewed by appropriately qualified professionals.
Dynamic Residency is based in Istanbul, Türkiye. The service is delivered in English and German through a cross-border professional network.

items.q: Who is the service designed for?
items.a: The service is designed for entrepreneurs, investors, internationally mobile families, returning Turkish citizens, international professionals, retirees and advisers whose decisions involve connected residence, income, company, asset or family questions.

items.q: Does Dynamic Residency recommend Türkiye to every client?
items.a: No. The purpose is to establish whether Türkiye works for the client's complete international position. Where the structure is not viable, the correct outcome may be not to proceed.

The process begins with a structured review of the available facts and objectives. Dynamic Residency identifies the relevant scope, information gaps, dependencies and questions requiring specialist input before recommending the next step.

cta.title: Establish Whether the Structure Works
cta.body: General information can explain Türkiye. Your situation requires an individual answer based on your residence history, income, companies, investments and family circumstances.

hero.title: Understand Türkiye's 20-Year Foreign Income Tax Exemption
hero.lead: For entrepreneurs, investors and internationally mobile families
hero.small: Verified legal basis: Article 20/D of the Turkish Income Tax Law and General Communiqué No. 333
hero.imageAlt: Businessman and child overlooking Istanbul skyline

framework.title: For Internationally Connected Individuals
framework.lead: Türkiye has introduced a significant personal income-tax exemption for eligible individuals who establish Turkish tax residence.
framework.body1: Under Article 20/D of the Turkish Income Tax Law, individuals who satisfy the statutory residence-history conditions may receive an exemption from Turkish income tax on income and gains genuinely obtained outside Türkiye for a statutory period of 20 years.
framework.body2: The provision applies to individuals who become resident in Türkiye from 1 January 2026 onwards. It is based on residence history, tax history and the source of the income — not on nationality. The legislation does not state a monetary ceiling for qualifying foreign income.
framework.noteBody: This is not a general “20 years tax-free” status. The exemption applies only where the individual, the income and the application process satisfy the legal requirements.
framework.cta: Read Article 20/D in the Official Gazette

rows.a: Natural persons who become resident in Türkiye and satisfy the applicable residence and tax-history conditions. The regime does not provide a corporate tax exemption.
Income and gains genuinely obtained outside Türkiye, subject to classification under Turkish source rules.
A statutory exemption period of 20 years.
Article 20/D does not state a monetary ceiling for qualifying foreign income.
Yes. The individual must apply to the competent Turkish tax office and obtain the prescribed Foreign Income Exemption Certificate.

incomeStreams.title: Income Streams That May Be Relevant
items: Dividends from foreign companies
Rental income from property outside Türkiye
Foreign interest and investment returns
Certain foreign capital gains
Certain foreign pension receipts
Other income arising from assets or activities genuinely located outside Türkiye
incomeStreams.outro: Each income stream must be classified separately. A foreign payer, foreign company or foreign bank account does not by itself make income foreign-source.

eligibility.title: The Four Questions That Determine Eligibility

items.title: Will You Become Resident in Türkiye?
items.desc: Article 20/D applies to individuals who are considered resident in Türkiye under Turkish tax law. A residence permit, property purchase or number of days spent in Türkiye may be relevant, but no single fact should be assessed in isolation. Domicile, physical presence, family circumstances and applicable double-tax treaties may all affect the final residence position. The individual must already be considered resident in Türkiye when applying for the exemption certificate.

items.title: What Is Your Previous Three-Calendar-Year History?
items.desc: As a general rule, the individual must not have had either a domicile in Türkiye or a disqualifying Turkish tax liability during the three calendar years preceding the year in which the individual becomes resident. Certain previous Turkish tax liabilities arising from Turkish rental income, investment income or capital gains do not automatically prevent qualification. Previous employment, commercial or professional activity in Türkiye may, however, affect eligibility and requires detailed review.

items.title: Where Was the Income Genuinely Obtained?
items.desc: The location of the payer or bank account is not decisive. The underlying asset, activity, employment, service, company distribution or transaction must be examined to determine whether the income was genuinely obtained outside Türkiye.

items.title: Can You Obtain the Certificate on Time?
items.desc: The exemption is not automatic. The application must generally be completed by the end of the calendar year in which the individual becomes resident in Türkiye. Individuals who become resident during the final two months of a calendar year may apply until the end of February of the following year. A late application can result in the exemption certificate being refused even where the other substantive conditions are satisfied.

sourceClassification.title: Practical Examples
items: Rent from property physically located outside Türkiye may be foreign-source.
A dividend from a non-Turkish company may be foreign-source, subject to the surrounding facts.
Professional services physically performed from Türkiye may remain Turkish-source even if the client and payment account are abroad.
Salary, management fees and business profits may require additional analysis of where the work is performed and where the business is managed.

whySignificant.eyebrow: Why the Exemption May Be Significant
whySignificant.title: Who May Find It Relevant
whySignificant.lead: For an eligible internationally connected individual, the exemption may substantially change the long-term economics of establishing residence in Türkiye.

cards.title: International Investors
cards.desc: Individuals receiving dividends, portfolio income, interest, foreign rental income or gains from assets held across several jurisdictions.

cards.title: Company Owners and Founders
cards.desc: Entrepreneurs retaining ownership in foreign companies after relocating to Türkiye. The personal exemption may be relevant to dividends or other qualifying foreign income, but it does not determine company residence, permanent establishment, salary taxation or departure-country exit tax.

cards.title: Returning Turkish Citizens
cards.desc: Citizenship does not by itself prevent qualification. A returning Turkish citizen may be eligible where the previous three-calendar-year residence and tax-history requirements are satisfied.

cards.title: Internationally Mobile Families
cards.desc: Families coordinating residence, investments, property, education, succession and long-term asset ownership across more than one country.

cards.title: Retirees
cards.desc: Individuals receiving pensions, rental income or investment income from outside Türkiye, subject to the source rules and the relevant double-tax treaty.

whatNot.eyebrow: What the 20-Year Exemption Does Not Do
whatNot.title: Not a Blanket Tax Exemption
items: Exempt income arising from Turkish property, Turkish companies or activities performed in Türkiye
Exempt every payment received from a foreign customer, employer or bank account
Apply to companies or remove corporate tax obligations
Remove VAT, payroll, stamp-duty or social-security obligations
End tax residence or reporting obligations in another country
Eliminate departure tax or exit tax in the country being left
Prevent a foreign company from becoming managed or taxable in Türkiye
Override a double-tax treaty
Correct a late or unsupported exemption application
whatNot.outro: Foreign taxes paid on income falling within the Turkish exemption cannot be credited against Turkish income tax. Expenses and costs connected with exempt income also cannot be used to reduce income that remains taxable in Türkiye.

individualVsCompany.eyebrow: The Exemption Belongs to the Individual
individualVsCompany.title: Personal and Company Positions Must Be Analysed Separately
individualVsCompany.body1: Article 20/D is a personal income-tax provision. A qualifying shareholder may potentially receive exempt foreign-source income while the company from which that income arises remains fully taxable in its own jurisdiction.
individualVsCompany.body2: The individual's exemption does not automatically protect the company from:
items: Turkish corporate residence
Permanent-establishment exposure
Payroll obligations
Transfer-pricing rules
Withholding taxes
Local accounting and reporting requirements
individualVsCompany.outro: For entrepreneurs and shareholders, the personal position and the company position must therefore be analysed separately.

profiles.title: How the Rule May Apply in Practice

cards.title: Foreign Investment Income
cards.desc: An eligible individual becomes resident in Türkiye while continuing to receive dividends from a foreign company and rental income from property outside Türkiye. These income streams may fall within Article 20/D where they are genuinely foreign-source, the individual satisfies the previous three-calendar-year test and the exemption certificate is obtained on time.

cards.title: International Entrepreneur
cards.desc: A founder relocates to Türkiye while retaining a foreign operating company. Foreign dividends may be relevant to the exemption, but salary, management activity, company residence, permanent establishment and departure-country exit tax must be examined separately.

cards.title: Remote Professional
cards.desc: A consultant lives and performs services from Türkiye for clients located abroad. The foreign location of the clients or payment account does not automatically make the income foreign-source. Income connected with work physically performed in Türkiye may remain taxable in Türkiye.

cards.title: Returning Turkish Citizen
cards.desc: A Turkish citizen returns after several complete calendar years abroad. The person may qualify if the statutory residence and tax-history conditions are satisfied. Citizenship is not the decisive test.

application.title: Plan the Certificate Process Before the Deadline
application.body1: The exemption-certificate process should be planned before or during the relocation — not after the relevant deadline has passed.
application.body2: The Turkish tax office may review:
items: The date on which the individual became resident in Türkiye
Residence and domicile during the preceding three calendar years
Previous Turkish tax registrations and income types
The nature and source of the foreign income
Supporting residence, tax and income documentation
The accuracy of the statements made in the application
application.outro: If it is later determined that the conditions were not satisfied, unpaid tax may be assessed together with penalties and interest.
application.noteBody: General Communiqué No. 333 sets out the administrative application and documentation framework for Article 20/D.
application.noteCta: Read General Communiqué No. 333 in the Official Gazette

widerDecision.title: The Turkish Exemption Is Only One Part of the Decision
widerDecision.intro: A successful relocation must work in every relevant jurisdiction. Before changing residence, the complete structure should be reviewed, including:
items: Current and future tax residence
Exit tax and departure reporting
Foreign companies and shareholdings
Location of management and decision-making
Investment and property income
Employment and professional activity
Social-security exposure
Family residence and succession
Applicable double-tax treaties
Timing of the Turkish exemption application
widerDecision.noteBody: The question is not simply whether Article 20/D exists. The question is whether it works for your income, companies, investments, family position and country of departure.

cta.eyebrow: Could the 20-Year Exemption Work for You?
cta.title: Review the Complete Structure
cta.body1: Dynamic Residency provides a structured review of your Turkish residence position, previous tax history, foreign income and wider cross-border structure.
cta.body2: We identify the questions that require regulated tax or legal input, coordinate the appropriate professionals and convert the results into a clear decision and implementation roadmap.
cta.small1: Discuss whether Türkiye and Article 20/D fit your wider cross-border structure.
cta.small2: Based in Istanbul | English and German service | Confidential cross-border process

terms.title: General Information Only
terms.body1: This page provides general information about Article 20/D of the Turkish Income Tax Law and General Communiqué No. 333. It does not constitute individual tax, legal or investment advice.
terms.body2: Eligibility and income-source classification depend on the facts of each case. Regulated tax and legal opinions should be provided or reviewed by appropriately qualified professionals.
terms.small: General information only — not individual tax or legal advice

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.section1Text: Our website, https://dynamicresidency.com (hereinafter: "the website") uses cookies and other related technologies (for convenience all technologies are referred to as "cookies"). Cookies are also placed by third parties we have engaged. In the document below we inform you about the use of cookies on our website.
privacyPolicy.section2Text: A cookie is a small simple file that is sent along with pages of this website and stored by your browser on the hard drive of your computer or another device. The information stored therein may be returned to our servers or to the servers of the relevant third parties during a subsequent visit.
privacyPolicy.section3Text: A script is a piece of program code that is used to make our website function properly and interactively. This code is executed on our server or on your device.
privacyPolicy.section4Text: A web beacon (or a pixel tag) is a small, invisible piece of text or image on a website that is used to monitor traffic on a website. In order to do this, various data about you is stored using web beacons.
privacyPolicy.section5Text: 5.1 Technical or functional cookies. Some cookies ensure that certain parts of the website work properly and that your user preferences remain known. By placing functional cookies, we make it easier for you to visit our website. This way, you do not need to repeatedly enter the same information when visiting our website and, for example, the items remain in your shopping cart until you have paid. We may place these cookies without your consent.
privacyPolicy.section6Text: When you visit our website for the first time, we will show you a pop-up with an explanation about cookies. As soon as you click on "Save preferences", you consent to us using the categories of cookies and plug-ins you selected in the pop-up, as described in this Cookie Policy. You can disable the use of cookies via your browser, but please note that our website may no longer work properly. Functional: The technical storage or access is strictly necessary for the legitimate purpose of enabling the use of a specific service explicitly requested by the subscriber or user, or for the sole purpose of carrying out the transmission of a communication over an electronic communications network.
privacyPolicy.section7Title: 7. Your rights with respect to personal data
privacyPolicy.section7Text: You have the following rights with respect to your personal data: You have the right to know why your personal data is needed, what will happen to it, and how long it will be retained for. Right of access: You have the right to access your personal data that is known to us. Right to rectification: you have the right to supplement, correct, have deleted or blocked your personal data whenever you wish. If you give us your consent to process your data, you have the right to revoke that consent and to have your personal data deleted. Right to transfer your data: you have the right to request all your personal data from the controller and transfer it in its entirety to another controller. Right to object: you may object to the processing of your data. We comply with this, unless there are justified grounds for processing. To exercise these rights, please contact us at the contact details at the bottom of this Policy.
privacyPolicy.section8Text: You can use your internet browser to automatically or manually delete cookies. You can also specify that certain cookies may not be placed. Another option is to change the settings of your internet browser so that you receive a message each time a cookie is placed. For more information about these options, please refer to the instructions in the Help section of your browser. Please note that our website may not work properly if all cookies are disabled. If you do delete the cookies in your browser, they will be placed again after your consent when you visit our websites again.
privacyPolicy.section9Text1: For questions and/or comments about our Cookie Policy and this statement, please contact us by using the following contact details:

termsAndConditions.title: Terms and Conditions
termsAndConditions.lastUpdate: The Terms & Conditions were last updated on March 1, 2024
termsAndConditions.section1Text: These Terms and conditions apply to this website and to the transactions related to our products and services. You may be bound by additional contracts related to your relationship with us or any products or services that you receive from us. If any provisions of the additional contracts conflict with any provisions of these Terms, the provisions of these additional contracts will control and prevail.
termsAndConditions.section2Text: By registering with, accessing, or otherwise using this website, you hereby agree to be bound by these Terms and conditions set forth below. The mere use of this website implies the knowledge and acceptance of these Terms and conditions. In some particular cases, we can also ask you to explicitly agree.
termsAndConditions.section3Text1: We or our licensors own and control all of the copyright and other intellectual property rights in the website and the data, information, and other resources displayed by or accessible within the website.
termsAndConditions.section3Text2: Unless specific content dictates otherwise, you are not granted a license or any other right under Copyright, Trademark, Patent, or other Intellectual Property Rights. This means that you will not use, copy, reproduce, perform, display, distribute, embed into any electronic medium, alter, reverse engineer, decompile, transfer, download, transmit, monetize, sell, market, or commercialize any resources on this website in any form, without our prior written permission, except and only insofar as otherwise stipulated in regulations of mandatory law (such as the right to quote).
termsAndConditions.section4Text1: Our website may include hyperlinks or other references to other party's websites. We do not monitor or review the content of other party's websites which are linked to from this website. Products or services offered by other websites shall be subject to the applicable Terms and Conditions of those third parties. Opinions expressed or material appearing on those websites are not necessarily shared or endorsed by us.
termsAndConditions.section4Text2: We will not be responsible for any privacy practices or content of these sites. You bear all risks associated with the use of these websites and any related third-party services. We will not accept any responsibility for any loss or damage in whatever manner, however caused, resulting from your disclosure to third parties of personal information.
termsAndConditions.section5Text1: By visiting our website, you agree to use it only for the purposes intended and as permitted by these Terms, any additional contracts with us, and applicable laws, regulations, and generally accepted online practices and industry guidelines. You must not use our website or services to use, publish or distribute any material which consists of (or is linked to) malicious computer software; use data collected from our website for any direct marketing activity, or conduct any systematic or automated data collection activities on or in relation to our website.
termsAndConditions.section5Text2: Engaging in any activity that causes, or may cause, damage to the website or that interferes with the performance, availability, or accessibility of the website is strictly prohibited.
termsAndConditions.section6Text1: You may register for an account with our website. During this process, you may be required to choose a password. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of passwords and account information and agree not to share your passwords, account information, or secured access to our website or services with any other person. You must not allow any other person to use your account to access the website because you are responsible for all activities that occur through the use of your passwords or accounts. You must notify us immediately if you become aware of any disclosure of your password.
termsAndConditions.section6Text2: After account termination, you will not attempt to register a new account without our permission.
termsAndConditions.section7Text: Do not submit any ideas, inventions, works of authorship, or other information that can be considered your own intellectual property that you would like to present to us unless we have first signed an agreement regarding the intellectual property or a non-disclosure agreement. If you disclose it to us absent such written agreement, you grant to us a worldwide, irrevocable, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use, reproduce, store, adapt, publish, translate and distribute your content in any existing or future media.
termsAndConditions.section8Text: We may, in our sole discretion, at any time modify or discontinue access to, temporarily or permanently, the website or any Service thereon. You agree that we will not be liable to you or any third party for any such modification, suspension or discontinuance of your access to, or use of, the website or any content that you may have shared on the website. You will not be entitled to any compensation or other payment, even if certain features, settings, and/or any Content you have contributed or have come to rely on, are permanently lost. You must not circumvent or bypass, or attempt to circumvent or bypass, any access restriction measures on our website.
termsAndConditions.section9Text1: Nothing in this section will limit or exclude any warranty implied by law that it would be unlawful to limit or to exclude. This website and all content on the website are provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis and may include inaccuracies or typographical errors. We expressly disclaim all warranties of any kind, whether express or implied, as to the availability, accuracy, or completeness of the Content. We make no warranty that:
termsAndConditions.section9List1: this website or our content will meet your requirements;
termsAndConditions.section9List2: this website will be available on an uninterrupted, timely, secure, or error-free basis.
termsAndConditions.section9Text2: Nothing on this website constitutes or is meant to constitute, legal, financial or medical advice of any kind. If you require advice you should consult an appropriate professional.
termsAndConditions.section9Text3: The following provisions of this section will apply to the maximum extent permitted by applicable law and will not limit or exclude our liability in respect of any matter which it would be unlawful or illegal for us to limit or to exclude our liability. In no event will we be liable for any direct or indirect damages (including any damages for loss of profits or revenue, loss or corruption of data, software or database, or loss of or harm to property or data) incurred by you or any third party, arising from your access to, or use of, our website.
termsAndConditions.section9Text4: Except to the extent any additional contract expressly states otherwise, our maximum liability to you for all damages arising out of or related to the website or any products and services marketed or sold through the website, regardless of the form of legal action that imposes liability (whether in contract, equity, negligence, intended conduct, tort or otherwise) will be limited to the total price that you paid to us to purchase such products or services or use the website. Such limit will apply in the aggregate to all of your claims, actions and causes of action of every kind and nature.
termsAndConditions.section10Text: To access our website and/or services, you may be required to provide certain information about yourself as part of the registration process. You agree that any information you provide will always be accurate, correct, and up to date.
termsAndConditions.section11Text: We are committed to making the content we provide accessible to individuals with disabilities. If you have a disability and are unable to access any portion of our website due to your disability, we ask you to give us a notice including a detailed description of the issue you encountered. If the issue is readily identifiable and resolvable in accordance with industry-standard information technology tools and techniques we will promptly resolve it.
termsAndConditions.section12Text: Access to the website from territories or countries where the Content or purchase of the products or Services sold on the website is illegal is prohibited. You may not use this website in violation of export laws and regulations of the United States.
termsAndConditions.section13Text: You may not assign, transfer or sub-contract any of your rights and/or obligations under these Terms and conditions, in whole or in part, to any third party without our prior written consent. Any purported assignment in violation of this Section will be null and void.
termsAndConditions.section14Title: 14. Breaches of these Terms and conditions
termsAndConditions.section14Text: Without prejudice to our other rights under these Terms and Conditions, if you breach these Terms and Conditions in any way, we may take such action as we deem appropriate to deal with the breach, including temporarily or permanently suspending your access to the website, contacting your internet service provider to request that they block your access to the website, and/or commence legal action against you.
termsAndConditions.section15Text: You agree to indemnify, defend and hold us harmless, from and against any and all claims, liabilities, damages, losses and expenses, relating to your violation of these Terms and conditions, and applicable laws, including intellectual property rights and privacy rights. You will promptly reimburse us for our damages, losses, costs and expenses relating to or arising out of such claims.
termsAndConditions.section16Text: Failure to enforce any of the provisions set out in these Terms and Conditions and any Agreement, or failure to exercise any option to terminate, shall not be construed as waiver of such provisions and shall not affect the validity of these Terms and Conditions or of any Agreement or any part thereof, or the right thereafter to enforce each and every provision.
termsAndConditions.section17Text: These Terms and Conditions will be interpreted and construed exclusively in English. All notices and correspondence will be written exclusively in that language.
termsAndConditions.section18Text: These Terms and Conditions, together with our privacy statement, constitute the entire agreement between you and Dynamic Works in relation to your use of this website.
termsAndConditions.section19Title: 19. Updating of these Terms and conditions
termsAndConditions.section19Text: We may update these Terms and Conditions from time to time. It is your obligation to periodically check these Terms and Conditions for changes or updates. The date provided at the beginning of these Terms and Conditions is the latest revision date. Changes to these Terms and Conditions will become effective upon such changes being posted to this website. Your continued use of this website following the posting of changes or updates will be considered notice of your acceptance to abide by and be bound by these Terms and Conditions.
termsAndConditions.section20Text: These Terms and Conditions shall be governed by the laws of the United States. Any disputes relating to these Terms and Conditions shall be subject to the jurisdiction of the courts of the United States. If any part or provision of these Terms and Conditions is found by a court or other authority to be invalid and/or unenforceable under applicable law, such part or provision will be modified, deleted and/or enforced to the maximum extent permissible so as to give effect to the intent of these Terms and Conditions. The other provisions will not be affected.
termsAndConditions.section21Text1: This website is owned and operated by Dynamic Works.
termsAndConditions.section21Text2: You may contact us regarding these Terms and Conditions by writing or emailing us at: info{'@'}dynamicworks.email
